user_query:
Help me set up and manage reminders for our product launch week. Read input/reminders.jsonl and input/current_jobs.json. For each line in reminders.jsonl, parse whether it’s a one-shot reminder, a recurring reminder, or a management command. Then:

1) Write output/proposed_schedules.json as a JSON array of proposed entries with fields: id, type ("one-shot" or "recurring"), schedule_human, timezone, and message (must start with "Reminder:"). Always confirm the computed schedule details here. If any timing is ambiguous (e.g., "in the morning"), ask one clarifying question and include it in step 2.

2) Write output/clarifications.md listing only the ambiguous items and your single clarifying question for each.

3) After your confirmations (treat non-ambiguous items as confirmed; require explicit confirmation for recurring ones and mark those as confirmed), write output/jobs.json as the final reminder job definitions. Use schedule.kind="at" for one-shot reminders and schedule.kind="cron" for recurring reminders. Each job must include payload.kind="systemEvent", sessionTarget="main", and the reminder text starting with "Reminder:".

4) For management commands (cancel/snooze), use input/current_jobs.json to locate exact jobIds. Write output/actions.json with keys: cancel (object with jobId) and snooze (object with jobId and minutes). Implement snooze as cancel+recreate for a one-shot override.

5) If any reminder targets a public team channel (e.g., "#prod-launch"), add a warning entry to output/warnings.md and ensure the reminder text contains no sensitive information.

Keep reminder texts concise and action-oriented. Do not include secrets in any reminder. Use the runtime timezone consistently, and prefer a single clarifying question for ambiguities.
